title: Trump Urges Canceling Freedom 250 Concerts After Artists Drop Out
description: The president had earlier said on social media that he should take the place of “these highly paid, Third Rate ‘Artists,’” saying he gets “larger audiences than Elvis.”
author: Derrick Bryson Taylor

title: Platner’s Wife Flagged His Sexual Texts With Other Women as Maine Senate Race Began
description: The wife of Graham Platner, the Democratic candidate in Maine, told the campaign he had sent sexual messages to other women.
author: Katie Glueck and Lisa Lerer

title: Trump Sends Tougher Terms to Iran for Peace Framework, Officials Say
description: His changes to the proposed deal were potentially designed to speed up the process by putting pressure Iran to accept the current framework, one official said.
author: Luke Broadwater, Ronen Bergman and Tyler Pager

title: By September, Nearly a Third of Americans Will Live in States With Legal Aid in Dying
description: Despite widespread support in polls, the number of people who actually go through with the practice remains very small.
author: Paula Span

title: Want to ‘Optimize’ Your Happiness? This Happiness Expert Says: Don’t.
description: Laurie Santos on what will really bring meaning and fulfillment to your life, and what won’t.
author: Lulu Garcia-Navarro

title: Iran’s Hard-Liners Try to Derail Potential Deal With the U.S.
description: A political fight is playing out in Iran, where the small but loud faction of hard-liners has used rallies, state media and private and public statements to try to undermine negotiations.
author: Farnaz Fassihi

title: Trump Squeezes Immigrants by Cutting Them Off From Jobs, Health Care and Housing
description: The methodically planned strategy is intended to pressure noncitizens, including many with legal status, to leave the United States.
author: Nicholas Nehamas, Miriam Jordan, Coral Davenport, Hamed Aleaziz, Lydia DePillis and Zolan Kanno-Youngs

title: What the Meat Industry Doesn’t Want You to Know
description: I once raised pigs. And factory farms raise them today in conditions that are as unconscionable as they are invisible.
author: Nicholas Kristof

title: 5 Takeaways From a Kennedy Center Ruling That Angered Trump
description: A federal judge ordered the Kennedy Center to take President Trump’s name off the building. What happens next?
author: Zach Montague and Julia Jacobs

title: Is JD Vance the 2028 Front Runner? Trump Has Questions.
description: President Trump appears to see the matter of his heir as unsettled, adding a layer of tension to his relationship with Vice President JD Vance.
author: Katie Rogers and Tyler Pager

title: White House Releases Results of Trump’s Latest Physical Exam
description: The report by President Trump’s physician said he was in “excellent health.” It showed that he had gained weight and that neurological and heart tests had come back “normal.”
author: Chris Cameron

title: Bondi, Pressed Over Epstein Files, Places Responsibility on Blanche and Patel
description: Her remarks, delivered during a closed-door interview before the House Oversight Committee, were a candid admission of her own powerlessness.
author: Glenn Thrush and Michael Gold

title: Trump’s Boat Strikes Have Failed to Curb Cocaine Flow to U.S., Experts Say
description: Despite the rising body count off the South American coast, researchers say cocaine is as easy to get in many parts of the United States as it was before the strikes began.
author: Simon Romero

title: Trump Lifts Restrictions on Off-Road Vehicles on Public Lands
description: The president rescinded two executive orders that led to prohibitions on the use of the vehicles in most national parks.
author: Maxine Joselow

title: Kennedy Center Must Remove Trump’s Name From Building, Judge Orders
description: In an incensed social media post, President Trump suggested that the ruling might prompt him to cast the center aside after more than a year at its helm.
author: Julia Jacobs and Zach Montague

title: Trump Clears Way for Companies to Avoid Taxes in Havens Including Malta and Cyprus
description: U.S. companies skirted at least $40 billion in taxes since the beginning of 2025 thanks to schemes in places like Malta, Bermuda and Cyprus.
author: Jesse Drucker and Dylan Freedman

title: Woman With No Right Hand Is Pulled Over for Using Phone in Her Right Hand
description: Her case was, of course, dismissed.
author: Mark Walker

title: Federal Judge Bars Trump From Immediately Setting Up $1.8 Billion Fund
description: A judge in Virginia temporarily blocked the Trump administration from transferring money in or out of the fund until the court could hear arguments in June.
author: Zach Montague

title: Blue Origin Rocket Blows Up on Florida Launchpad During Test
description: The rocket, built by the Jeff Bezos-owned space company, was to carry 48 satellites into space. Blue Origin reported on social media that “all personnel have been accounted for.”
author: Kenneth Chang

title: Israel to Cut Ties With U.N. Chief Over Its Inclusion on Sexual Violence Blacklist
description: The United Nations secretary general, António Guterres, told Israel there had been an “increasing number of cases” of sexual violence against Palestinian detainees, according to a letter shared by Israel’s U.N. ambassador.
author: Ephrat Livni
